paper_title: The Distal and Local Volcanic Ash in the Late Pleistocene Sediments of the Termination I Interval at the Reykjanes Ridge, North Atlantic, Based on the Study of the Core AMK-340

methods.description: Process for collecting and describing the sediment core AMK-340 from the Reykjanes Ridge, North Atlantic, to analyze its litho- and biostratigraphy.

steps.description: The sediment core AMK-340 was obtained during the 4th cruise of the Russian research vessel 'Akademik Mstislav Keldysh'.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core AMK-340 from Reykjanes Ridge

steps.description: A visual lithological description of the core exhibited no signs of the volcanic material.
steps.searchable_summary: perform visual lithological description of sediment core

methods.description: Development of an age model for the sediment core AMK-340 using AMS 14C-datings and litho- and biostratigraphy.

steps.description: The linear interpolation between four AMS 14C-datings helped to develop an age model of the core AMK-340.
steps.searchable_summary: develop age model using AMS 14C-datings and interpolation

steps.description: Calibration of the 14C-datings in the calendar age data was made in the CALIB 7.1.0 computer software using the MARINE13 calibration scale.

methods.description: Micropaleontological analysis of the core AMK-340 sediments to identify eruptive shards and analyze their chemical composition.

steps.description: During the micropaleontological analysis of the core AMK-340 sediments under the stereomicroscope and transmitted light microscope, we could recognize a remarkable admixture of the eruptive shards.
steps.searchable_summary: identify eruptive shards in sediment core using microscopes

steps.description: Two instruments examined the chemical composition of the shards: a scanning electron microscope CamScan MV2300 and VEGA3 TESCAN with EDS.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze chemical composition of shards using SEM and EDS
